channel.title: SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: Woodinville Half
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Kirkland Marina Park, 25 Lakeshore Plz Dr, Kirkland, WA

Kirk-Wood Half is now the Woodinville Half Hello runners, We know many of you have been looking forward to the Kirk-Wood Half Marathon, and we appreciate your understanding as we share an important update regarding the event. Unfortunately, we have had to change the route for the Kirk-Wood Half due to ongoing construction on the Kirkland Cross Corridor Trail. The good news: the half marathon will still take place on Sunday, March 8 — just with an updated route. The event will now start and finish at Wilmot Park, which was originally planned as the finish line. This eliminates the need for participant shuttles and allows us to offer a flat, fast, and fun course. We are incredibly grateful for the support and flexibility shown by the City of Woodinville and King County Parks in making this possible. At this time, our focus is on delivering an exceptional race experience on March 8. The City of Kirkland has indicated a willingness to permit the event on a future date, and we are exploring the possibility of hosting the Kirk-Wood Half Marathon later this year. Important Updates: The event will now be called the Woodinville Half Marathon. All participants will receive a free slice of pizza from The Pizza Coup post-race . All participants will still receive a full-zip hooded sweatshirt with registration. We will be updating the design. View the new course map: https://www.mapmyrun.com/routes/view/6667331427 Thank you for your continued support, we can’t wait to see you at the start line! Sincerely, Grant Harrington Owner Snohomish Running Company

Event details

item.title: WA Concealed Carry Class at Wildlife Committee of
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Wildlife Committee of Washington - WCWShootingSports - Kenmore Range, 1031 228th St SW, Bothell, WA

This 4-hour comprehensive training course is designed to educate individuals on the laws and regulations surrounding the carrying of concealed firearms. This class covers essential topics such as the use of force, firearms safety, and the proper handling and storage of firearms, as well as the specific laws and any requirements for obtaining permits in Washington, Oregon, Arizona, and Utah. Upon completion of the class, attendees will be eligible to apply for the above-mentioned permits. You will also receive a WA - HB-1143 Gun Safety Certificate. DATES and TIMES are subject to change, please view our website for the most up to date and accurate class info. https://www.legalheat.com/classes/in-person/concealed-carry-class-at-wildlife-committee-of-washington-bothell-wa/?utm\_source=facebook&utm\_medium=event

Event details

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

Event details

item.title: ToDos Bike Garage -- Bike Repair Co-op
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Come work on bikes on Sundays! Please call/text ahead \(323.926.8054\) to confirm ToDos Bike Garage will be open on a given Sunday. Volunteer run. ToDos Bike Garage is open for bike repair, tune-up, and flat fixes. We have all the basic bike tools and are ready to work on bikes together! We're happy to teach you how to maintain your bike, and help you get it working right. It's free to come work on your bike, and we do have some parts available, but you may need to buy your own parts if something comes up. Follow @todos\_garage on Instagram!

Event details

item.description:

item.title: WWE Monday Night RAW
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Location: Climate Pledge Arena, 334 1st Ave N, Seattle, WA

Please visit our website to view the Arena Guide with Bag Policy and Prohibited Items list. Sale Dates and Times: Public Onsale : Tue, 13 Jan 2026 at 10:00 AM VIP Package Presale : Mon, 12 Jan 2026 at 10:00 AM VIP Package Onsale : Tue, 13 Jan 2026 at 10:00 AM Venue Presale : Mon, 12 Jan 2026 at 10:00 AM WWE Presales : Mon, 12 Jan 2026 at 10:00 AM

Event details

item.title: Spring Sports Night
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Skyline High School, 1122 228th Ave SE, Sammamish, WA

Parents and Guardians of Spring Sports Spartan athletes, please join us on Monday, March 9th for Spring sports night. All Parents and Guardians will begin in the Spartan Gym at 6:30 p.m. and will be followed by breakout sessions immediately by team with the Head Coach. Breakout sessions will last between 30 and 60 minutes depending upon the sport. Booster Club Board Members will also be present at this night to share information on how Boosters supports athletes across these sports teams. Go Spartans!

Event details

item.title: Coffee Connections (March)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Active Mill Creek, 122 128th St SE, Everett, WA

March’s COFFEE CONNECTIONS is HOSTED at: Active Martial Arts Address: 122 128th St SE, Everett, WA 98208 Please join your fellow Mill Creek Chamber members and guests to our monthly member-hosted event where businesses get to network, learn about each other, and connect on a personal and casual level! Guests welcome also! Always an exciting new Mill Creek Chamber event; we’ll be hosted at new location each month, so if you have a venue that you’d like to gain more exposure from, please let us know! If you’re a guest, we welcome you to learn more about the benefits of membership. When available, our Chamber Director will be present to discuss, and when not; Networking Committee members can assist as well. Have some coffee, make some connections. • March 10th, 2026 • 8am-9:30am

Event details

item.title: Coffee Connections
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Active Martial Arts Mill Creek, 122 128th St SE, Ste B, Everett, WA

Guests welcome also! Always an exciting new Mill Creek Chamber event; we’ll be hosted at new location each month, so if you have a venue that you’d like to gain more exposure from, please let us know! item.title: Plant Relocation Party
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 12 PM

We will be moving the plants from the Perimeter Trail to the Native Plant Learning Garden. This work will support the Native Plant Restoration Project of the Park. The walk is only a short 5 min with wheelbarrows. Please bring shovels if you have any. Dress warmly, rain or shine we will be there!

Event details

item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Fremont Abbey Arts Center, 4272 Fremont Ave North, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Washington Huskies Baseball vs. Seattle University Redhawks Baseball
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Husky Ballpark, Walla Walla Rd, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Eastside Coffee Outside Every Wednesday Morning
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Rotary Central Station Picnic Shelter, 1 Railroad Avenue, Kirkland, WA

Eastside Coffee Outside Every Wednesday Morning Drop by to join us ANY Wednesday, rain or shine, for a chat, a snack, and something hot to drink. No RSVP necessary. You'll find us Every Wednesday, 7:00 AM to 9:30 AM Rotary Central Station \(the picnic shelter\) 1 Railroad Ave, Kirkland, WA 98033 Anna brings her fresh-baked vegan muffins to share. Dylan often brews different varieties of loose-leaf tea to share. Kurt brews regular and decaf coffee and brings hot water, tea bags, and hot cocoa packets. Bring your pup for a treat, too! We're here from 7-9:30am, but you can stop by for as much or as little of that time as works for you. It's on the Cross Kirkland Corridor, so it's easily accessible for walkers, joggers, bikers, and by car. There are reusable mugs available, or bring your own. Hope to see you ANY or EVERY Wednesday morning!

Event details

item.title: North End SHG Park Day!
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Location: St Edward State Park, 6990 NE 145th, Kenmore, WA

Welcome to SHG's North End weekly parkday group! We meet every Wednesday during the school year, rain or shine, at St. Edward State Park. The play is unstructured and child-led, and often includes large field games like capture the flag or freeze tag, or smaller groups adventuring around the large playground together. All homeschool ages are welcome, and our current age ranges are usually 5 to 16. Because it is a state park, the parking lot requires a yearly Discover Pass, or a day pass. Annual passes will be increasing from $30 to $45 on October 1st. We want park day to be accessible for all homeschoolers, so if parking costs is a barrier, please contact Christine Jones for options. More information on the park can be found here: https://parks.wa.gov/find-parks/state-parks/saint-edward-state-park Parking passes can be found here: https://store.discoverpass.wa.gov

Event details

item.title: Restoration Event | Native Plant Nursery
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 12 PM

Monthly Native Plant Nursery Work Party Calling all native plant lovers! We're looking for volunteers to join us each month to help us care for the plants in our native plant nursery. Every year we host a c ouple thousand native plants in the nursery until they're ready to be put in the ground in parks across the island. This will be a great oppor tunity to learn more about native plants, learn about basic plant care, and help us ensure our plants healthy before being put in their new homes. Tasks may include: weeding potted plants, up-potting or repotting native plants, plant organizing, plant propagation, weeding gravel areas, tool care, nursery pot maintenance, and watering. When: Thursday, March 12th, 12pm-2pm \(join us on your lunch break!\) Where: Luther Burbank Park, Caretakers building What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: long pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/B4AFB Opens in new w indow PDF Download Word Download Excel Download PowerPoint Dow nload Document Download

Event details

Event details

item.title: Lichen Walk
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Dr. Katherine Glew. Have you always wondered what lichens are and why they are found on your trees, rhododendrons and azaleas? Lichens are harmless to your plants and add aesthetic value to your garden. Learn about common lichens found in an urban environment and what they tell us about air quality, forest health, geology and why they are indicators of changes in the climate, ecosystems, and geology. You will leave with a fresh understanding about lichens and a user-friendly chart that lists lichens found in your neighborhood. This walk will be on trails with various surfaces and may include gravel, mulch, or pavement. Please dress for the weather and wear comfortable walking shoes. $28 BBGS members/$40 nonmembers.

Event details

item.title: Conservation in the City
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: West Fork Tibbets Trailhead, 2533 NW Stoney Creek Dr, Issaquah, WA

Join Issaquah councilmember and IATC President, Kelly Jiang, to learn about the history of conservation in Issaquah, new land conservation initiatives, and more. Additionally, with daylight savings, it will be light until 7 - perfect for weekday evening hikes! TRAIL: West Fork Tibbetts Trailhead PARKING: Street parking available Distance: 3.5 miles Duration: 2.5 hours Elevation Gain: 900 ft. Registration required. Pets allowed.

Event details

item.title: Saturday Flow + Yin Yoga
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 8 AM

This class will leave you feeling physically and energetically balanced! Practice begins with 30 minutes of dynamic movement. Emphasis on synchronizing breath with poses to create an intense internal heat and purifying sweat that detoxifies the body. The last 30 minutes of practice, poses are held for several minutes focusing on finding increased mobility in the joints – spine, hips and shoulders. Each pose will aim to access the deep layers of fascia to release tension holding areas on the body. \*Drop-in for $25

Event details

item.title: 2026 Kirkland Shamrock Run (5k, 10k and Lil Leprechaun Run)
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Kirkland Marina Park, 25 Lakeshore Plz Dr, Kirkland, WA

A Kirkland and Eastside tradition is put on my Running Company, Cascade Stride. This is the run that everyone talks about weeks after the race! Signing up early saves LOTS of money. Come join Lake Washington Physical Therapy and Cascade Stride by "wearing the green" for a fun family fun run. The event will start and end at the Marina Park in Kirkland, WA. Run along Lake Washington Blvd and enjoy views of Lake Washington and the Seattle skyline. Registration fees

Event details

item.title: Highway 9 Bridge Planting Party
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Description Join us at this restoration site along South Skagit Highway near the Highway 9 bridge. From 9am-12pm we'll plant native trees and shrubs to restore riparian habitat at this riverside site. These plants will help restore the Skagit River and improve habitat for salmon and many other animals. Bring a club, group and all your friends- all ages welcome! Please dress for the weather, and wear shoes that can get muddy. We'll provide gloves, tools and hot drinks/snacks! Click the link to register today!

Event details

item.title: Shamrock 3K Run
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Town Hall, 1119 Eighth Avenue (at Seneca Street), Seattle, WA

Get ready to lace up your sneakers and join us for a festive Shamrock 3K Run, where we'll be running, laughing, and celebrating together!

Event details

item.title: Women's Day Out: Shop, Sip & Self-Care
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: The Winemakers Reserve, 15902 Woodinville-Redmond Rd NE, Suite D, Woodinville, WA

Join us for a day of pampering, wellness, fitness and shopping as we celebrate the importance of self-care! ✨ Women’s Day Out: Sip, Shop & Self-Care ✨ Join us on Saturday, March 14 at The Winemakers Reserve in Woodinville for a day designed to refresh your mind, body, and soul!   💆‍♀️ Pamper yourself with spa \+ wellness services 🧠 Discover resources to support your mental & physical health 🛍️ Shop unique finds from local businesses 🎁 Enter exciting giveaways & door prizes 🎉 Enjoy fun surprises throughout the day 🧁 Indulge in delicious sweet treats 🎶 Relax or groove to live music   …and we are bringing back fitness & wellness classes for our VIP ticket holders! Come solo, with your girlfriends, spouse, or a loved one—you’ll leave feeling refreshed and inspired!   While this event is designed with women in mind, all are welcome to join the celebration.   BOGO Tickets available through January 20th! Regular Admission: $13.80 VIP Admission: $49.64 VIPs will receive a complimentary glass of wine, access to four classes, and a swag bag valued at $200\+. Interested in being a vendor? We’d love to hear from women-focused businesses like you! Apply at https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdh9BT04UZ0j3RnIfOmOou\_pR6vS3knbxFfPp4mJ2ZTZ6oJfw/viewform?fbclid=PAZXh0bgNhZW0CMTEAc3J0YwZhcHBfaWQMMjU2MjgxMDQwNTU4AAGnpyixJNHPOOzJHYreNrcM7WCCFclxIWxTeIYeuu8ClTmuhBD7Ft4QlzU3brg\_aem\_0jikthn12Lbt8b8XyhFyEQ

Event details

item.title: Family Saturday
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Olympic Sculpture Park, 2901 Western Ave, Seattle, WA

Spend a fun-filled morning with your family learning about art and the environment then get creative with a drop-in art activity led by a local artist. This program takes place outside at the Olympic Sculpture Park on the covered north terrace of the PACCAR Pavilion. Walk-ins welcome on a first-come, first-served basis.

Event details

item.title: Restoration Event | Pioneer Park
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 10 AM

Pioneer Park Clean Up! Join us for a Forest Steward led wo rk party in Pioneer Park SE. For this work party volunteers will be removi ng ivy that is blanketing the forest floor. Removing ivy helps to increase the biodiversity of park by making room for native plants to sprout up. I f left unchecked, ivy will grow over our native plants and eventually pull them to the ground. Your help in removing ivy will play a pivotal role i n creating a healthier future for the park! No experience necessary, all ages welcome. Please note: we require participants aged 14 and under to volunteer with a parent or guardian at a ratio of 1 adult to 3 kids. When: Saturday, March 14th, 10am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park SE, a map will be provided for directions to the specific work site. What to bring : Dress for the weather in clothes you don't mind getting dirty \(think: lo ng pants, boots or sneakers\). Bring water. Small snacks, gloves, and tools will be provided. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/CWRCR

Event details

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

Event details

item.title: Daybreak Star Community 5K Run and Walk
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th \(37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend, starting at 9:00 AM\) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center \(5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle, WA 98199\) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group, please email communications@unitedindians.org

Event details

item.title: ToDos Bike Garage -- Bike Repair Co-op
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 11 AM

Come work on bikes on Sundays! Please call/text ahead \(323.926.8054\) to confirm ToDos Bike Garage will be open on a given Sunday. Volunteer run. ToDos Bike Garage is open for bike repair, tune-up, and flat fixes. We have all the basic bike tools and are ready to work on bikes together! We're happy to teach you how to maintain your bike, and help you get it working right. It's free to come work on your bike, and we do have some parts available, but you may need to buy your own parts if something comes up. Follow @todos\_garage on Instagram!

Event details

item.title: Meditation, Breathwork, Cold 3rd Sunday of the month at Origins Unity
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Origins Unity LLC, 229 NW 56th Street, Seattle, WA

Meditation, Breathwork, Cold Plunge & Community! Guided session w/ Certified Yoga, QiGong,Trauma Sensitive, Meditation & Breathwork Teacher

Event details

item.title: Softball vs. Sacramento State
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Logan Field, Seattle, WA

item.title: Bicycle Roadside Repair - Prepare for the Unexpected
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Shoreline Tool Library, 16610 Aurora Avenue North, Shoreline, WA

Hands-on class for on-the-go bike fixes. Learn to repair flats, broken spokes and more so you can keep riding no matter the distance.

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:

Event details

item.title: Issaquah Alps Trail Running Club #227
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Mountainside Drive Trailhead, Mountainside Dr SW, Issaquah, WA

Meet at the Bullitt Fireplace Trailhead on Mountainside drive. There's plenty of parking along Mountainside drive. Bring a headlamp for the run. Join us after the run for our weekly potluck. Bring a potluck dish with a serving utensils, and your own dish and utensil. Route: Main Route: This route is 6mi with 1600ft of gain. The run starts with most the vert up to the microwave, and heads down Chybinski trail. Route: https://www.strava.com/routes/3290114707713132698 Strava Event: https://www.strava.com/clubs/993715/group\_events/3462646694539309928

Event details

item.title: Washington Huskies Baseball vs. Saint Martins University Saints Baseball
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Husky Ballpark, Walla Walla Rd, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Trail Explorers - Kelly's Brook Trail
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Farmer's Market: 245 Freshwater Road. Upper parking lot, 4749 Ballard Ave NW, Seattle, WA

Join the Trail Explorers on Wednesday, March 18, at 10:30 a.m. They will be exploring Kelly's Brook Trail, and the meeting location is the Farmer’s Market: 245 Freshwater Road. Upper parking lot. This trail is 4 km and will take approximately 60-75 minutes to complete. It is rated a 3 on the difficulty scale \(on a 1-5 scale, with 1 being the easiest and 5 being the hardest. For more information please visit: https://www.stjohns.ca/en/recreation-culture/trail-explorers.aspx This initiative provides a wonderful opportunity to explore the beautiful trails throughout the city, promoting health, wellness, and community connection. Please contact healthyprograms@stjohns.ca for further information.

Event details

item.title: Audubon Bird Walk
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Learn more about the birds at the Bellevue Botanical Garden. Join Eastside Audubon for a monthly walk that explores the birds and their habitat in the Garden. This one-mile walk will be on trails with various surfaces and may include gravel, mulch, or pavement. Please dress for the weather and wear comfortable walking shoes. Parking in the lot is free. From the parking lot enter the garden through the breezeway and turn left. Meet your guide in the covered Gathering Place. This walk is for any level of birder and no experience is necessary. Bring binoculars if you have them. There will be some available to loan for the walk. Each walk is $5 per person. Registration is required.

Event details

item.title: Saint Edward State Park Restoration Work Party
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Do you want to give back to Saint Edward State Park? Are you looking for meaningful ways to support local natural spaces? Come join us! Join us for a day of stewardship and discovery at Saint Edward State Park! The park is home to rich flora, fauna, and history and with increased visitation, it needs our collective care more than ever. All ages 8 \+ welcome; no experience needed. Work happens rain or shine, please dress for the weather. What to bring \(required\): Rain Jacket Closed toe shoes Water Hand pruners or loppers \(we have some but could always use more\) Gardening Gloves \(we will provide extra if you do not have your own\)

Event details

item.title: Restoration Event with WA State Parks
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: May Valley Loop Trailhead, Issaquah, WA

Event details

item.title: Mindfulness Walk | Pioneer Park
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 11 AM

Mindfulness Walk Taking inspiration from the Japanese idea of shinrin-yoku \(forest bathing\), we will be hosting a series of mindfulness walks in Pioneer Park. Mindfulness walking in the forest has shown to have a myriad of benefits from improving heart health to reducing stress \(https://www.npr.org/2023/08/22/1195337204/a-guide-to-forest-bathing\). Forest Steward Marilyn Cornwell \(https://www.soulscapes.us/\) will be lea ding an hour-long guided walk through the park. Please join us if your cur ious about mindfulness, want to learn more about and experience healing powers of being in nature, and/or if you want to deepen your connection with Pioneer Park and others. When: Thursday, March 19th, 11am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park NW, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes and shoes you will comfortable walking and standing in, as well as water. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/9XTJA

Event details

item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 10 AM

Event details

item.title: Forterra Fridays: March 2026
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Crestview Park, 16200 42nd Avenue South, Tukwila, WA

Forterra Fridays: March 2026 Friday, Mar. 20, 2026, 1:00 pm to 3:30 pm We’re excited to invite you to volunteer with us at Crestview Park, a beloved community gathering space in Tukwila featuring dog play areas, trails, and natural landscapes. Join us on March 20, from 1:00-3:30 PM, to restore essential habitat by removing ivy and Himalayan blackberries that threaten our local native trees, shrubs, and groundcover. Established native plants benefit local wildlife, improve water quality, and enhance air quality.  You will also gain valuable insights into ecological restoration and our innovative approach to land conservation. We’ll start with a walk of the site and have restoration professionals on hand to answer questions about plants, restoration, and habitat management.  What we are doing: Crestview Park provides critical habitat restoration opportunities alongside its popular amenities. By removing invasive ivy and blackberries, we ensure that native plants can thrive, supporting the park’s ecological health while maintaining the community spaces that make this park so valued by Tukwila residents.  What to bring: Please dress for the weather \(in layers to stay warm/dry\). Snacks, drinks, tools, and gloves will all be provided. Where to park: Park in the Crestview Park parking lot \(16200 42nd Avenue South\). Signs direct you from the parking lot to where we will meet. Date and Time: Friday, March 20, 2026, 1:00 –3:30 PM  Register and learn more here.

Event details

item.title: Forterra Fridays
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Crestview Park, Seattle, WA

Forterra Fridays: March 2026 Friday, Mar. 20, 2026, 1:00 pm to 3:30 pm We'reexcited to invite you to volunteer with us at Crestview Park, a beloved community gathering space in Tukwila featuring dog play areas, trails, and natural landscapes. Join us on March 20, from 1:00-3:30 PM, to restore essential habitat by removing ivy and Himalayan blackberries that threaten our local native trees, shrubs, and groundcover. Established native plantsbenefitlocal wildlife, improve water quality, and enhance air quality. You will also gain valuable insights into ecological restoration and our innovative approach to land conservation.We'llstart with a walk of the site and have restoration professionals on hand to answer questions about plants, restoration, and habitat management. What we are doing: Crestview Park provides critical habitat restoration opportunities alongside its popular amenities. By removing invasive ivy and blackberries, we ensure that native plants can thrive, supporting the park's ecological health while maintaining the community spaces that make this park so valued by Tukwila residents. What to bring: Please dress for the weather \(in layers to stay warm/dry\). Snacks, drinks, tools, and gloves will all be provided. Where to park: Park in the Crestview Park parking lot \( 16200 42nd Avenue South\). Signs direct you from the parking lot to where we will meet. Date and Time: Friday, March 20, 2026, 1:00 -3:30 PM Register and learn more here .

Event details

item.title: Green Issaquah: Restoration Event at Park Pointe
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: SE Evans St, Issaquah, WA

Join the Issaquah Alps Trails Club and Green Issaquah on the third Saturday of each month for invasive species removal events at Park Pointe! We will be removing invasive species such as Himalayan blackberry bushes to prepare for our fall planting event. These invasive plants inhibit the growth of native plants, and removing them will ensure the longevity and health of our newest additions. This event is part of our ongoing efforts to help meet the Issaquah Climate Action Plan goal of 55% tree canopy cover by 2035. When and Where Date & Time: Saturday, March 21st, 2025, 9 AM - Noon Where: Park Pointe Where to Park Parking for Park Pointe is available along SE Evans Street, which is overflow for Issaquah High School and the parking for the Sportsman Club \(see inset picture map below\). Click here for parking directions. Where to Meet We will be meeting at the end of the dirt road at the trailhead. What to Bring Bring your own gloves if you have them. There will be gloves available for those who don't have their own. Please dress in layers and in clothing you don't mind getting dirty. Closed-toed shoes are required. Long sleeves and pants, especially denim, are strongly recommended. Bring a full water bottle and snacks. Don't forget to bring your sunscreen! Point of Contact Anne Newcomb \(anne@annenewcomb.net\) and Matt LaBelle \(mattlabelle@hotmail.com\), your work party leaders. Registration required. You can check out our Event FAQs on our website.

Event details

item.title: DeBays Slough Planting Party
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Description Join us for planting at the Debays Slough Reserve on Saturday, March 21st. Join us 9am-noon to continue restoring the riparian zone of DeBay's Slough off Francis Road in Mount Vernon. Please dress for the weather; we will provide work gloves and tools, and closed-toe shoes or mud boots. Click the link to register today!

Event details

item.title: SAM Body + Mind: Activity Days
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Olympic Sculpture Park, 2901 Western Ave, Seattle, WA

Experience a full spectrum of grounding, creative, and wellness-focused practices with SAM Body \+ Mind: Activity Days. Designed to rejuvenate both inner and outer worlds, each session offers a mix of mindful movement, quiet reflection, hands-on creativity, and holistic care. Participate in gentle Qigong to awaken your energy, enjoy replenishing moments with acupuncture, or settle into the calm of silent reading in a peaceful shared space. Explore your artistic side with natural dye and art-making workshops that nourish connections to yourself and the natural world. Come for one activity or stay for the full experience-each offering invites you to slow down, reconnect, and care for your body and mind. 8:30 am | Doors open 9-10 am | Tai Chi/Qigong With Sensei Jody Garcia 10 am-1 pm | Workshops Art workshop with NW Expressive and Creative Arts Institute Natural dyes workshop with Earth Corps Acupuncture with Dr. Yanzi Jiang, Dr. Milo Jackson, and Dr. Nikeeta Shah noon-2 pm | Musical performance Ambient Vinyl DJ set performed by DJ Raica 1-2 pm | Silent reading With West Seattle Book Club Food and drinks Food and beverages will be available for purchase from Shooby Doo Catering in the PACCAR Pavilion. Presenting Sponsor

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

Event details

item.title: South Lynnwood Park Stewardship Event
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Scriber Lake Park, 5562 196th St SW, Lynnwood, WA

City of Lynnwood Parks welcomes volunteers of all abilities who want to contribute to the restoration and conservation of our parks in South Lynnwood Neighborhood. The goal of each event is to provide a safe, enjoyable, and effective hands-on experience for you to help improve the health of your urban parks. These monthly events, which will be held on the third weekend of each month, will involve removing invasive plants, help with restoration plantings, and picking up garbage. Events are 100% outdoors and will generally be held ‘rain or shine’ but may be canceled or relocated if weather or site conditions are unsafe such as high winds, flooding, snow, lightning, or unsafe air quality \(smoke\).

Event details
